text:
Vict (ビクト, Bikuto) is a high-ranking soldier in both the Royal Army of the Kakin Empire and 1 st Prince Benjamin Hui Guo Rou's personal army, and one of the fifteen employees that 1 st Prince Benjamin Hui Guo Rou is allowed to bring aboard. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 Under Benjamin's orders, he joined the guards of 12 th Prince Momoze Hui Guo Rou Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 and later 9 th Prince Halkenburg Hui Guo Rou, where he was attacked by Halkenburg. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 The soul of Halkenburg's man currently resides in Vict's body together with Vict's own soul, and control of Vict's body alternates between the two, with priority going to Vict. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

Appearance

Vict has thick eyebrows, a thick mustache, and extra-long sideburns, nearly forming a muttonchops-style mustache. He dons the standard uniform worn by the men of Benjamin's guard, comprising of a light-colored puffer jacket, light-colored tactical pants, and dark combat boots. He wears an earpiece in his left ear. 

Background

Vict's authorization to act as a royal guard indicates that he is an official soldier of the Royal Army. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 He is also a member of Benjamin's personal army, and is a graduate of the Kakin Royal Military Academy. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 373 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Vict, among the other thirteen soldiers working under Prince Benjamin, is present during Balsamilco's explanation before the journey's start. 

Later, Prince Benjamin orders his private soldiers, to both act as bodyguards and spies to all the other princes, and to report any information regarding the Princes' Guardian Spirit Beasts and the Hunters' abilities. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 As Prince Momoze rests in her bedroom, Vict, Nipaper, Bladge, and Laroc stand guard outside her room, while Tuffdy patiently waits to assassinate the Prince. After Prince Momoze's murder, Vict, along with the other bodyguards are taken into custody by the Kakin Army to be interrogated about the murder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 

Vict is subsequently freed and eventually assigned to Room 1009. In response to an attack by 9 th Prince Halkenburg, Vict uses his Nen ability, believed by Benjamin to be able to be used both offensively and defensively, to no effect. He manages to warn Benjamin about the latter's bow before being defeated. When Steiner, along with his associate Peuckert and the Kakin Justice Bureau, arrives at Prince Halkenburg's room, Vict is nowhere to be seen. 

On the morning of the eleventh day, Vict appears in the Justice Bureau Prosecution and Courthouse Area building behind Balsamilco he walks through a narrow hallway. Balsamilco is shocked to see him, and Vict begins using military sign language to communicate to him that he had discovered the secret to Halkenburg's ability. Vict distracts Balsamilco long enough for Halkenburg to use Grimmal the Dissonance and shoot an arrow at Balsamilco, forcing Balsamilco to be possessed by Halkenburg himself. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

Later, after "Balsamilco" (Halkenburg's soul, inside Balsamilco's body) reports to Benjamin that Halkenburg had vomited and was taken to the hospital on Tier 3, "Halkenburg" mentally reviews his ability, thinking about how their experiment with Shikaku and Sumidori had helped them some theories, and how their previous experimentation with Vict let Halkenburg prepare for the case where he himself was transferred, and the case where the possessor's body dies. 

Abilities & Powers

Vict has received military training. 

Nen

Vict is a Nen user skilled enough to have developed a Nen ability. However, his superior, Balsamilco, believes him to be less knowledgeable than Hunters in that regard. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 364 

Manga Appearances

Battles

Trivia

The second name of Vict's Nen ability is the same as that of one of the ending themes for the 2011 anime adaptation.
